text: GULF OF OMAN: USS George H.W. Bush F/A-18 Super Hornets dropped precision munitions down the smokestacks of Iranian-flagged tankers M/T Sea Star III and M/T Sevda May 8. Both vessels disabled. Iran claims 10 sailors wounded, 5 missing. CENTCOM blockade tally now 58 commercial vessels turned around.
verificationNote: TWZ visual analysis + NPR + Stars and Stripes corroborated.

text: ASSESSMENT: Iran's May 8 missile/drone/small-boat attack on three US destroyers (Truxtun, Mason, Rafael Peralta) is consistent with IRGC Cmdr Vahidi's strategy of constraining diplomacy by escalating, blocking pragmatist factions in Tehran. Trump's 'love tap' framing creates rhetorical room to absorb hits without abandoning ceasefire — both sides appear to want plausible deniability.
verificationNote: ISW/CTP iran-update May 5; Trump quotes per Fox News.

text: ANALYSIS: China is positioning to broker an end to the war on terms favorable to Iran. Wang Yi-Araghchi Beijing meeting (May 6) and the proposed 'four shared homelands' framework lay groundwork for a Chinese-mediated reopening of Hormuz that would entrench Iranian transit authority via PGSA.
verificationNote: FDD published May 6 analysis on China-Iran de-escalation.

text: TANKER STATUS: Around 31 Iranian tankers (~53M bbl) still trapped in the Gulf since blockade Day 1. April monthly transit total just 191 vessels — 5% of pre-war monthly. Lloyd's confirms zero transits since May 4 except for sanctioned vessels in U-turn. Insurance war-risk premiums ~1% of hull value per 7-day voyage (8x pre-war).
verificationNote: WEF article + Lloyd's List + Military Times confirmed transit and insurance figures.

text: LEBANON FRONT: IDF executed 85+ strikes on Hezbollah weapons storage and production sites in 24 hours — most intense since April truce. Underground weapons production facility in Beqaa Valley destroyed. Hezbollah's claimed Mt. Meron drone swarm strike marks first overt cross-border claim since the April ceasefire. 19+ killed in Lebanon May 9.
verificationNote: Times of Israel + Jerusalem Post + Washington Times confirmed strike details.

text: UAE STRIKE: UAE Defense Ministry confirms its air defenses engaged 2 Iranian ballistic missiles + 3 drones May 8 — 3 injured by debris. Reflects Iran continuing escalation against US Gulf partners despite formal ceasefire framework, consistent with PressTV claims of damage to US naval assets in Hormuz (denied by US).
verificationNote: PBS NewsHour confirmed UAE Defense Ministry statement.

displayName: Institute for the Study of War
text: DIPLOMATIC: Iran President Pezeshkian held a 2.5-hour in-person meeting with new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei on the US 14-point peace MOU. CNN: US intel assesses Mojtaba is 'still shaping strategy' via written statements while remaining out of public view since wartime injuries. Pezeshkian rebrands US blockade as 'extension of military action.'
verificationNote: CNN reporting May 8 citing US intelligence sources.

text: MARKETS: Brent settled $101.29 (+26.9% pre-war), WTI $95.60 (+26.6%) May 8 — reversed week's ~7% ceasefire-hope sell-off after the Gulf of Oman tanker incident. AAA gas $4.530, slight pullback from $4.55 mid-week. S&P 500 hit record 7,398.93 (+0.84%) on Iran response anticipation. 10Y Treasury 4.36%.
verificationNote: CNBC, AAA, FXDailyReport confirmed price levels.
